page: Vultr vc2-4c-8gb is an x86_64 architecture cloud server belonging to the Cloud Compute family. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores using shared Intel Xeon processors running at 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 160 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. No GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance operates in the lower tiers of compute capability. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1304.17 and 5030.23 respectively, placing them in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory performance shows a stark contrast between cached and uncached operations; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 367.64 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the bottom 25% at 24.41 GB/sec and 19.55 GB/sec. Database and decompression benchmarks also reflect constrained throughput, with Passmark database operations scoring 637.68 and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaching 14.88 GB/sec.

Due to its shared CPU allocation and modest performance profile, the vc2-4c-8gb offers a highly cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute resources. The inclusion of a 160 GB NVMe SSD provides fast local storage access relative to the processing limits. This server is best suited for lightweight applications such as basic static web serving, minor Redis caching tasks, development environments, and low-intensity background utility processing.
description: Vultr vc2-4c-8gb is an x86_64 cloud server in the Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 160 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data positions this server in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 5030.23 and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth at 24.41 GB/sec. While cached small-block memory reads perform at an average tier of 367.64 GB/sec, overall processing and database throughput remain modest, as shown by a Passmark database score of 637.68. Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for non-critical workloads. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as static web serving, development environments, and basic utility applications.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared CPU instance featuring NVMe SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads and development environments.

8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Bundled 160 GB NVMe SSD local storage

Suited for static web serving, development, and lightweight utility tasks
